metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_3958
metadata.comment:
Rationale: De Jong et. al demonstrate that RIM1 binds to the plasma membrane of the presynaptic active zone.
- In STED microscopy, RIM colocalizes with Bassoon, a well established active zone marker. This colocalization is true for both endogenous RIM as well as RIM1 rescue constructs, which will be used in the remainder of the study (Figure 1 G-M)
- The C2B domain binds to PIP2, a phospholipid that is present in the plasma membrane. The interaction is demonstrated with NMR spectroscopy, lipid cosedimentation and lipid cofloatation assays (Figure 3A-F). Additionally, a mutation is identified that abolishes PIP2 binding (Figure 4).
- The PIP2-binding mutation in the C2B domain inhibits the function of RIM1 C2B in action-potential induced synaptic transmission (Figure 5).. Experimental description: RIM1/2 conditional knockout mice were previously described (Kaeser et al., 2011) and were maintained as homozygote lines in which the Rims1 gene (RRID:IMSR_JAX:015832 (Kaeser et al., 2008)) and the Rims2 gene (RRID:IMSR_JAX:015833 (Kaeser et al., 2011)) contained essential exons flanked by loxp sites. All animal experiments were approved by the Harvard University Animal Care and Use Committee.
